inline_review:
commit 7a5cfe2184463ef6d694cfaafb908c4749f360e7
Author: David Carlier <devnexen@gmail.com>

mm/memcontrol: fix obj_cgroup leak in mem_cgroup_css_online() error path

This commit fixes a memory and reference leak by killing the initial
percpu_ref when obj_cgroup_alloc() fails during the NUMA node loop in
mem_cgroup_css_online(). It adds rcu_replace_pointer() and
percpu_ref_kill() to the error path to properly tear down the allocations.

> diff --git a/mm/memcontrol.c b/mm/memcontrol.c
> --- a/mm/memcontrol.c
> +++ b/mm/memcontrol.c
[ ... ]
> @@ -4172,6 +4173,9 @@ static int mem_cgroup_css_online(struct cgroup_subsys_state *css)
>  free_objcg:
>  	for_each_node(nid) {
>  		struct mem_cgroup_per_node *pn = memcg->nodeinfo[nid];
> +		objcg = rcu_replace_pointer(pn->objcg, NULL, true);
> +		if (objcg)
> +			percpu_ref_kill(&objcg->refcnt);
>  
>  		if (pn && pn->orig_objcg) {

This isn't a bug, but is the redundant NULL check for pn still needed?

The new code unconditionally dereferences pn during rcu_replace_pointer():

    objcg = rcu_replace_pointer(pn->objcg, NULL, true);

Immediately after, the pre-existing code defensively checks for pn:

    if (pn && pn->orig_objcg) {

Since mem_cgroup_css_online() is only invoked after mem_cgroup_css_alloc()
has successfully allocated nodeinfo for all nodes, pn is guaranteed to be
non-NULL in this context. While the unconditional dereference introduced by
the patch is perfectly safe, leaving the defensive check immediately after it
might incorrectly imply pn could be NULL. Could the pn check be removed
for clarity?

>  			obj_cgroup_put(pn->orig_objcg);
>  			/*
>  			 * Reset pn->orig_objcg to NULL to prevent
>  			 * obj_cgroup_put() from being called again in
>  			 * __mem_cgroup_free().
>  			 */

Can __mem_cgroup_free() crash due to a NULL pointer dereference of pn?

If alloc_mem_cgroup_per_node_info(memcg, node) fails for any NUMA node
during mem_cgroup_alloc(), it leaves memcg->nodeinfo[node] as NULL,
jumps to the fail label, and calls __mem_cgroup_free(memcg).

Inside __mem_cgroup_free(), the code iterates over all possible nodes and
unconditionally executes:

    struct mem_cgroup_per_node *pn = memcg->nodeinfo[node];
    ...
    obj_cgroup_put(pn->orig_objcg);

For the node where the allocation failed (and any subsequent unallocated
nodes), pn will be NULL, and evaluating pn->orig_objcg directly
dereferences pn.

This regression was introduced in commit 098fad3e1621 and remains
unaddressed in the current patch. Should a check for if (!pn) be added
before accessing its members?

>  			pn->orig_objcg = NULL;
>  		}
>  	}
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"__mem_cgroup_free() unconditionally dereferences pn->orig_objcg, leading to a kernel crash if mem_cgroup_alloc() fails.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"1. In `mem_cgroup_alloc()`, if `alloc_mem_cgroup_per_node_info(memcg, node)` fails for any NUMA node, it leaves `memcg->nodeinfo[node]` as NULL, jumps to the `fail` label, and calls `__mem_cgroup_free(memcg)`.
2. Inside `__mem_cgroup_free()`, the code iterates over all possible nodes and unconditionally executes `struct mem_cgroup_per_node *pn = memcg->nodeinfo[node];` followed immediately by `obj_cgroup_put(pn->orig_objcg);`.
3. For the node where the allocation failed (and any subsequent unallocated nodes), `pn` will be NULL. The inline evaluation of `pn->orig_objcg` directly dereferences `pn`, resulting in a kernel panic due to a NULL pointer dereference. This severe regression was introduced in commit 098fad3e1621 and remains unaddressed in the current patch. A check for `if (!pn)` must be added before accessing its members."},{"problem":"Inconsistent and redundant NULL check for `pn` in the `free_objcg` error path.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"In the `free_objcg` error path, the new code executes `rcu_replace_pointer(pn->objcg, NULL, true)`, which unconditionally dereferences `pn`. Immediately after, the pre-existing code defensively checks `if (pn && pn->orig_objcg)`. Because `mem_cgroup_css_online()` is only invoked after `mem_cgroup_css_alloc()` has successfully allocated `nodeinfo` for all nodes, `pn` is guaranteed to be non-NULL in this context. While the unconditional dereference introduced by the patch is perfectly safe and will not cause a crash, leaving the defensive `if (pn)` check immediately after it is confusing and incorrectly implies `pn` could be NULL. The redundant `pn` check should be removed for clarity."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 7a5cfe2184463ef6d694cfaafb908c4749f360e7
